metadata.status: All computational work paused. 350+ experiments, 669B+ configs, ALL NOISE.
notes: K4 CT is 97 chars, IC=0.0361, all 26 letters present, statistically consistent with random.
Multi-objective oracle threshold: 24/24 crib + Bean PASS + quadgram > -4.84/char + IC > 0.055 + 3+ non-crib words of 7+ chars + semantic coherence.
Scoring thresholds: NOISE<=6, STORE>=10, SIGNAL>=18, BREAKTHROUGH=24+Bean.

scripts.name: e_ledger_01_systematic_elimination.py
scripts.purpose: Systematic elimination ledger: covers all cipher families (Caesar/ROT, simple substitution, Vigenere, transposition, etc.) with elimination proof or remaining gap

scripts.name: e_poly_01_polyalphabetic_analysis.py
scripts.purpose: Polyalphabetic cipher analysis of K4: Kasiski, IC-per-period, mutual IC, statistical tests for polyalphabetic structure

scripts.name: e_stat_01_missing_tests.py
scripts.purpose: K4 statistical profiling missing tests: Kappa/Friedman IC-per-period, DFT spectral analysis, autocorrelation with MC baseline

source_modules.purpose: Single source of truth for CT, cribs, Bean positions, and score thresholds. Self-verifying at import.
key_constants: CRIBS: EASTNORTHEAST at pos 21-33, BERLINCLOCK at pos 63-73

source_modules.purpose: Alphabet models for keyword-mixed and standard alphabets.
Text sanitization and letter/number conversion utilities.
Frozen dataclass experiment configuration with SHA256 fingerprinting.
Crib scoring and implied key computation from fixed crib positions.
Bean equality and inequality constraint verification.
Monoalphabetic consistency and self-encryption checks.
Canonical anchored and free-position scorer combining all scoring signals.
Threshold-based crib match scoring and signal classification.
Index of Coincidence computation (full text and per-period).
Log-probability quadgram scorer for English plaintext quality.
Position-free crib scorer: searches for EASTNORTHEAST and BERLINCLOCK anywhere in text. Added for first-principles audit (2026-02-26).
Vigenere, Beaufort, and Var Beaufort encrypt/decrypt/key-recovery.
All standard transposition permutation generators and application utilities.
Polybius 5x5 square generation and Bifid encrypt/decrypt.
Multi-stage transform pipeline composition (Sub+Trans+Sub etc.).
WAL-mode SQLite database wrapper for result persistence.
Run manifest and JSONL writer for experiment artifact persistence.
Top-level evaluation pipeline: runs all scoring functions on a candidate.
Parallel sweep runner with checkpointing for large configuration spaces.
Experiment worker functions for block and full transposition sweeps.
Hypothesis data model with status tracking and research question linking.
Novel hypothesis generators: running key, date keys, transform recombinations.
Hypothesis triage: quick-filters new hypotheses before full sweep.
SQLite-backed novelty ledger for hypothesis deduplication across sessions.
Text corpus ingestion from files and URLs.
Egyptological text normalizer: transliteration-aware alpha extraction.
9-variant spelling generator for Egyptological corpus texts.
CLI entry point: doctor, sweep, reproduce, novelty, report commands.

scoring_functions.name: score_candidate
scoring_functions.description: THE canonical anchored scorer. Scores a candidate plaintext against all K4 constraints.

fields.crib_score: 0-24: total crib matches at fixed positions
fields.bean_passed: bool: Bean equality AND inequality constraints both satisfied
fields.ic_value: float: Index of Coincidence of plaintext

scoring_functions.name: score_candidate_free
scoring_functions.description: Position-free variant: searches for EASTNORTHEAST and BERLINCLOCK anywhere in text (not fixed positions). Added for first-principles audit (2026-02-26).
scoring_functions.notes: Used when crib position uncertainty is high or for audit cross-validation.

scoring_functions.name: score_cribs
scoring_functions.description: Simple crib match count at fixed positions (0-24). Fast filter before full scoring.

scoring_functions.name: score_cribs_detailed
scoring_functions.description: Detailed breakdown of crib matches: position-by-position pass/fail for all 24 crib characters.

scoring_functions.name: ic
scoring_functions.description: Index of Coincidence. English text ~0.065, random ~0.038. K4 CT IC=0.0361 (random-consistent).

scoring_functions.name: ic_by_position
scoring_functions.description: Per-position IC for period analysis. Non-significant for all periods 2-26 in K4.

scoring_functions.name: NgramScorer.score
scoring_functions.description: Total log-quadgram probability for text. English text returns higher (less negative) values.

scoring_functions.name: NgramScorer.score_per_char
scoring_functions.description: Log-quadgram score normalized per character. English ~-3.08, random ~-6.09, BREAKTHROUGH threshold: >-4.84.

scoring_functions.name: verify_bean
scoring_functions.description: Full Bean constraint verification: checks equality (k[27]=k[65]) and all inequality constraints. Returns detailed BeanResult with per-constraint pass/fail.
scoring_functions.notes: Bean EQ is variant-independent: CT[27]=CT[65]=P, PT[27]=PT[65]=R for any Vigenere/Beaufort/VarBeaufort.

scoring_functions.name: verify_bean_simple
scoring_functions.description: Quick Bean check: returns True only if all Bean equality + inequality constraints pass.

scoring_functions.name: evaluate_candidate
scoring_functions.description: Full pipeline entry point: applies cipher transform, scores result, persists if above STORE threshold.

disproofs_found.reason: CT has 2 E's; cribs EASTNORTHEAST+BERLINCLOCK require 3 E's at fixed positions. Impossible under any single-layer transposition.
Full pairwise Bean impossibility: all 276 pairwise constraints applied. ALL periods 2-26 directly eliminated.

disproofs_found.method: Bean impossibility for periodic key + ANY transposition
disproofs_found.reason: Periods {2-7,9-12,14,15,17,18,21,22,25} eliminated for ANY transposition + periodic key combination. Surviving set: {8,13,16,19,20,23,24,26}.
Algebraic impossibility for n=2,3,4 (matrix determinant constraints). n>4 blocked since 97 is prime.
Bifid 5x5/6x6: 26 letters impossible in 5x5; Trifid: IC constraints; ADFGVX: parity (97 odd); Playfair/Two-Square/Four-Square: odd length 97 blocked.
Structural maximum: PT-autokey max 16/24, CT-autokey max 21/24. Both provably below breakthrough threshold.
Bean-ELIMINATED: 38*delta≡0 mod 26 → delta in {0,13} only (degenerate — constant or two-value key).
Bean-ELIMINATED: 0/676 (a,b) pairs survive Bean constraint.
Bean-ELIMINATED: 0/676 seed pairs survive Bean constraint.
EAST appears twice in cribs (pos 21-24 and 30-33) but maps to different CT sequences FLRV and GKSS → non-constant shift [1,25,1,23]. Identical words must produce identical CT under pure nomenclator.
9/14 CT letters at crib positions map to 2+ distinct PT letters — impossible under homophonic decryption (each CT maps to exactly one PT).
Bean-ELIMINATED: zero orderings at w5 and w7 map positions 27 and 65 to the same column (required for Bean EQ). P(Bean EQ) = 0 at these widths.
Max 13/24 across all 720 orderings (noise, corrected p=0.485). Statistically indistinguishable from random.
Max 13/24 across all 40320 orderings — UNDERPERFORMS random expectation.
Max 14/24 across exhaustive sweep — UNDERPERFORMS random expectation.
Max 14/24 across sampled orderings (large width). All underperform random.

disproofs_found.method: Simple transposition families (cyclic, reverse, affine, block reversal, rail fence, single swaps)
disproofs_found.reason: 14,035 permutations tested; max 13/24 (noise).
2.96M compositions tested; max 15/24 (noise). 667B extended: max 18/24 (dragnet_v4) — all noise.
Max 15/24 (noise).
0% Bean pass for AMSCO/Nihilist at these widths — Bean EQ is structurally impossible.
ZERO candidates at small period products. Product space exhausted.
ZERO viable candidates across all tested configurations.

disproofs_found.method: Running key from 73+ Gutenberg books (47.4M chars English)
disproofs_found.reason: 462 EAST matches found; 19 pass Bean-EQ; ZERO pass full 24-position crib filter. Under identity transposition, all English Gutenberg literature is eliminated.
Cumulative corpus 130.7M+ chars: ZERO full matches under identity transposition.

disproofs_found.method: Egyptological corpus (57M chars, 9 variants) + columnar transposition widths 5-11
disproofs_found.reason: 1.19B configurations tested (44M perms × 3 cipher variants × 9 corpora). Zero hits ≥18/24.
Single and double CW/CCW rotations, pad 0-5, 1016 total configs tested. Max 4/24, ALL Bean FAIL.
377 configurations: clock face, city ordering, route ciphers. Max 6/24 (noise).
50,819 configurations (checkerboard, diagonal, 50K random). Max 2/24 (noise).

disproofs_found.method: Webster family names as Vigenere key (DRUSILLA etc.)
disproofs_found.reason: Bean-incompatible at period 8 (pos 27→S, pos 65→R, S≠R). Max 5/24.
CT uses all 26 letters; straddling checkerboard requires digits (10 symbols), structurally blocked.
97 CT letters → 48.5 PT letters (not integer). Non-integer plaintext length is impossible.

disproofs_found.method: K3-style compound cipher (Vigenere+Columnar) applied to K4
disproofs_found.reason: 0 full matches using K3's documented method with all reasonable key variants.
ALL physical reversal/mirror variants tested: ALL NOISE.

disproofs_found.method: EAST constraint applied to all tested corpora (identity transposition)
disproofs_found.reason: EAST gap-9 differential filter + Bean EQ: 462 EAST hits in 47.4M chars → 19 Bean-EQ → 0 full 24-position. All corpora eliminated under identity transposition.
Frequency analysis: K4 CT letter distribution incompatible with mono-substitution of English (chi-squared test). Bean EQ implies PT[27]=PT[65]=R, constraining mono.
All 25 shifts tested; none produce English plaintext.
Structural impossibility given Bean EQ constraint: best 5/24.
Bean-impossible at discriminating periods. LCG key generation cannot satisfy Bean EQ at periods 1-8.

promising_leads.hypothesis: Running key from unknown text + non-identity transposition
promising_leads.description: 73.7M chars eliminated under identity transposition, but with any non-identity transposition the problem becomes massively underdetermined. Unknown text + non-columnar trans cannot be eliminated computationally.
promising_leads.constraint_satisfaction: Model survives all known constraints. C(97,24)≈5e21 permutation DOF.
next_actions: Narrow via K5 constraints (shared coded words at same positions)
Bayesian prior on text type based on Sanborn 1986 Egypt trip

promising_leads.description: 13 monoalphabetic DOF absorb all constraints. SA finds trivial 24/24 solutions but all degenerate (mono collapse, IC=0.23-0.30). Model cannot be confirmed or eliminated.
next_actions: Constrain mono using letter frequency priors
Require IC > 0.05 to filter degenerate solutions

promising_leads.hypothesis: Bespoke physical/procedural cipher (coding charts, non-mathematical method)
promising_leads.description: Sanborn's statements: 'not a math solution', 'creativity', 97-day connection to Webster, Egypt trip 1986, coding charts auctioned for $962.5K. The coding charts (Scheidt letter, maquette) provide non-public key material. This is the hypothesis class that BEST fits all available evidence.

promising_leads.hypothesis: Homophonic substitution + very specific bespoke transposition
promising_leads.description: 1.6M transpositions tested (structured+columnar+random). P(0 contradictions among 9) < 1e-6 for random trans. Best achievable: 1 contradiction remaining. Would require extremely specific bespoke transposition not covered by any standard type.
next_actions: Targeted search for bespoke transpositions resolving last contradiction

promising_leads.description: Non-periodic key, Bean-compatible in principle, but ~26^3 × 97! parameter space makes it computationally intractable. Periods 2,3,5,6,7,9,10 eliminated; periods 4,8,13+ remain underdetermined.
next_actions: Constrain using K5 shared coded words

promising_leads.hypothesis: Pre-ENE segment (positions 0-20) uses different cipher or key
promising_leads.description: Positions 0-20 have IC=0.067 (English-like) vs positions 21-97 IC=0.035 (random-like). May indicate a different key or cipher method for the opening segment.

promising_leads.hypothesis: K5 constraints — K5 is 97 chars, shares coded words at same positions as K4
promising_leads.description: K5 confirmed by Sanborn (Aug 2025): 97 chars, 'similar but not identical coding', shares some coded words at same positions as K4. K5 CT is unknown — external information needed.
next_actions: Cross-K4/K5 joint constraint solver (once K5 CT available)

promising_leads.description: Sanborn designed Kryptos with physical S-curve copper sheeting and pool. The projection of the S-curve onto the pool may encode information geometrically. Untestable without precise sculpture geometry measurements.
next_actions: Hirshhorn physical inspection when museum reopens from renovation

promising_leads.description: In K3, CT YAR maps to PT WLD (UNDERGRUUND→UNDERGROUND). If K4 has similar post-decryption transformations, the solved PT may still require additional processing.
next_actions: Document all known Kryptos post-decryption transformations

promising_leads.hypothesis: External information: K5 CT, Smithsonian archives (sealed 2075), decoded coding charts
promising_leads.description: Kobek-Byrne (Sept-Oct 2025) discovered scrambled PT strips in Smithsonian archives — sealed until 2075. Auction buyer (anonymous) holds complete K4 archive. Sanborn health declining. These external sources are the highest-value information inputs.

missing_capabilities.description: No K5 CT scanner exists because K5 CT is unknown. Would be the single highest-value tool if K5 CT becomes available.
blockers: K5 CT unknown — requires Sanborn communication or Smithsonian archives (sealed 2075)

missing_capabilities.capability: Physical geometry solver for S-curve projection
missing_capabilities.description: No computational geometry tool for the S-curve/pool optical projection hypothesis. Requires precise 3D measurements of the Kryptos sculpture.
blockers: Requires physical access and laser scanning equipment

missing_capabilities.description: Simulated annealing consistently produces degenerate 24/24 solutions (mono collapse, IC=0.23-0.30). No non-degenerate adversarial PT explorer that enforces IC > 0.05 and non-degenerate mono mappings.
missing_capabilities.implementation_notes: Needs: IC > 0.05 hard constraint, mono bijection enforcement, minimum vowel ratio constraint (>25%)

missing_capabilities.description: No solver that jointly constrains K4 and K5 using their shared coding structure. Could dramatically narrow the key space once K5 CT is known.
The novelty ledger partially addresses deduplication, but 350+ scripts have been written without systematic cross-referencing. Many near-duplicate experiments likely exist.
missing_capabilities.implementation_notes: Novelty ledger (src/kryptos/novelty/ledger.py) exists but is not retroactively applied to legacy scripts

missing_capabilities.capability: Non-Western script corpus scanner (Arabic, hieroglyphic digital texts)
missing_capabilities.description: Sanborn's Egypt trip (1986) and Egyptological interest may involve Arabic or transliterated texts not covered by existing Egyptological pipeline. No Arabic OCR or hieroglyphic digital corpus integration.
missing_capabilities.implementation_notes: Egyptian hieroglyphics available in digital form (Thesaurus Linguae Aegyptiae). Arabic sources require separate transliteration pipeline.

missing_capabilities.capability: OCR pipeline for physical documents (coding charts, maquette)
missing_capabilities.description: The coding charts auctioned for $962.5K contain the actual key material. No OCR pipeline exists for extracting text from physical document photographs of these charts.
blockers: No public images of chart contents available

missing_capabilities.capability: Bayesian hypothesis ranking based on accumulated evidence
missing_capabilities.description: No Bayesian framework ranks remaining hypotheses by posterior probability given all 350+ experiment results. Would allow optimal allocation of future computational resources.
missing_capabilities.implementation_notes: Would require formalizing likelihood models for each experiment outcome and updating priors

missing_capabilities.capability: Running key search for columnar transposition widths > 11
missing_capabilities.description: E-EGYPT-01 covers Egyptological corpus + columnar w5-11. Widths 12+ are not covered for Egyptological or any other corpus. C(97,12) orderings are large but still searchable with Bean pre-filtering.
missing_capabilities.implementation_notes: Bean-EQ filters ~95.4% at widths 8-11, likely similar at w12+. Extend E-EGYPT-01 to w12-15.
